{{Shrine |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2年}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45年10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3年11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 穂積裕昌『伊勢神宮の考古学』[[雄山閣]]、2013年7月5日、208p. ISBN 978-4-639-02274-9 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[御井神社]]
== External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0211.html 下御井神社（しものみいのじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会

== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year }} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>

Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5年}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year }} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

{{Shrine |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year }} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year }} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

{{Shrine |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year }} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year }} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭}} (October 19) }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year }} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year }} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 穂積裕昌『伊勢神宮の考古学』[[雄山閣]]、2013rd year 7月5日、208p. ISBN 978-4-639-02274-9 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} * [[御井神社]]

The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=draft}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市|12=draft}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承|12=draft}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year|12=draft}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=draft}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year|12=draft}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承|12=draft}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year|12=draft}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=draft}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year|12=draft}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

{{Shrine |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 19) }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=draft}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市|12=draft}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承|12=draft}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year|12=draft}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=draft}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year|12=draft}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft}} (October 19) }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=draft}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市|12=draft}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft}}, the highest-ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承|12=draft}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year|12=draft}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=draft}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year|12=draft}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 穂積裕昌『伊勢神宮の考古学』[[雄山閣]]、2013rd year 7月5日、208p. ISBN 978-4-639-02274-9 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * [[御井神社]]

ISBN 978-4-639-02274-9 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[御井神社]]

[[sn:Shimo-no-Mii Shrine]] [[ja:下御井神社]] [[en:Shimo-no-Mii Shrine]]
<!--Shimo-no-Mii Shrine-->

{{Shrine |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 19) }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市|12=draft|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市|12=draft|12=draft}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft|12=draft}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承|12=draft|12=draft}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省|12=draft|12=draft}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "伊勢市の神社" to "Shrines in Ise City"
revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg|270px]] |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=draft|12=draft}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (October 19) }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=draft|12=draft}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|ja|伊勢市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft|12=draft}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|ja|玉垣|12=draft|12=draft}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|ja|松阪市|12=draft|12=draft}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa|ja|加古川市|12=draft|12=draft}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=draft|12=draft}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=draft|12=draft}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=draft|12=draft}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Japanese era)|ja|永承|12=draft|12=draft}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|Meiji 5|ja|明治5th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Divinities (Japan)|ja|教部省|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)|lt=Ministry of Religion|qq= (Japan)|12=draft|12=draft}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=draft|12=draft}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|Taishō 2|ja|大正2nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Ise-shi Station|ja|伊勢市駅|12=draft|12=draft}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|JR Central|ja|東海旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|ja|近鉄山田線|12=draft|12=draft}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|ja|近畿日本鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|12=draft|12=draft}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|12=draft|12=draft}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=draft|12=draft}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=draft|12=draft}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 穂積裕昌『伊勢神宮の考古学』[[雄山閣]]、2013rd year 7月5日、208p. ISBN 978-4-639-02274-9 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[御井神社]]
== External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0211.html 下御井神社（しものみいのじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{神道 横}}
