ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-639-02274-9 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[御井神社]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213708]] Wikidata name: Shimonomii Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.485389|N|136.7035|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchi-no-Miya|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>

{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|qq=}} (in [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka]]) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]]).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a [[:en:Suijin|water god]].<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanomiya|1=Taka-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431075}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of [[:en:Eishō (Heian period)|Eishō (Japanese era)]] (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] (July 1872), the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] South Exit (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]) or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)]] (operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]]).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]]). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213708]] Wikidata name: Shimonomii Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.485389|N|136.7035|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchi-no-Miya|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|qq=}} (in [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka]]) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]]).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a [[:en:Suijin|water god]].<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanomiya|1=Taka-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431075}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of [[:en:Eishō (Heian period)|Eishō (Japanese era)]] (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] (July 1872), the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] South Exit (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]) or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)]] (operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]]).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]]). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮』[[学生社]]、昭和45th year 10月20日重版、251p. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 穂積裕昌『伊勢神宮の考古学』[[雄山閣]]、2013rd year 7月5日、208p. ISBN 978-4-639-02274-9 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[御井神社]]

The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] South Exit (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]) or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)]] (operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]]).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]]). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine}} {{wikidata link|Q17213708}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Water wells in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213708]] Wikidata name: Shimonomii Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg |location = [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]], [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]] (October 19) |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.485389|N|136.7035|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]].
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchi-no-Miya|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the [[:en:Tamagaki|Tamagaki]] (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|qq=}} (in [[:en:Matsusaka|Matsusaka]]) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} (in [[:en:Kakogawa, Hyōgo|Kakogawa]]).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a [[:en:Suijin|water god]].<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanomiya|1=Taka-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431075}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of [[:en:Eishō (Heian period)|Eishō (Japanese era)]] (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] (July 1872), the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] South Exit (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]) or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)]] (operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]]).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]]). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of [[:en:Eishō (Heian period)|Eishō (Japanese era)]] (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of [[:en:1872|Meiji 5]] (July 1872), the [[:en:Ministry of Religion (Japan)|Ministry of Religion]] transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of [[:en:1913|Taisho 1]] (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise-shi Station]] South Exit (JR side) on the [[:en:Sangū Line|Sangū Line]] (operated by [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|JR Central]]) or the [[:en:Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)]] (operated by [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]]).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]]). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]] via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17213708]] Wikidata name: Shimonomii Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 19) |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.485389|N|136.7035|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchi-no-Miya|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|qq=}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanomiya|1=Taka-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431075}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Heian period)|en|Eishō (Heian period)|WD=Q1193549|lt=Eishō (Japanese era)}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} (operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchi-no-Miya|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|qq=}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanomiya|1=Taka-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431075}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Heian period)|en|Eishō (Heian period)|WD=Q1193549|lt=Eishō (Japanese era)}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} (operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchi-no-Miya|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>

{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|qq=}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god}}.<ref name="gk"/>

The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanomiya|1=Taka-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431075}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Heian period)|en|Eishō (Heian period)|WD=Q1193549|lt=Eishō (Japanese era)}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} (operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).

There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine]][[ja:下御井神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Shimo-no-Mii Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 1.jpg |location = {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17223204}} |Latitude Degrees = 34 |Latitude Minutes = 29 |Latitude Seconds = 7.4 |Longitude Degrees = 136 |Longitude Minutes = 42 |Longitude Seconds = 12.6 |Map = Japan Mie |deity = Shimo-no-Mii Guardian deity |type = {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} affiliated shrine |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = None |Other Names = Wakamiya (少宮) |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|en|Kannamesai Festival|WD=Q11255045|lt=Kannamesai}} (October 19) |location = {{ill|Toyokawachō|WD=Q17223204}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.485389|N|136.7035|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchi-no-Miya|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|qq=}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanomiya|1=Taka-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431075}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Heian period)|en|Eishō (Heian period)|WD=Q1193549|lt=Eishō (Japanese era)}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} (operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>

[[File:Shimo-no-Mii-no Shrine 2.jpg|thumb|Shrine cover structure]]
'''Shimo-no-Mii Shrine''' (下御井神社, *Shimo-no-Mii no Jinja*) is an affiliated shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It is located within the grounds of the Outer Shrine in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō, Ise|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō, Ise|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}.
The shrine is situated to the right of the stone steps leading to the {{ill|Taka-no-Miya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taka-no-Miya|qq=}}, and to the left when facing towards {{ill|Tsuchi-no-Miya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchi-no-Miya|qq=}}.<ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 59.</ref><ref name="ic">Ise Culture Publishing (2008): p. 37.</ref> It is also known by the alternate name '''Wakamiya''' (少宮).<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/>
== Overview ==
{{ill|Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|ja|下御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} is located within the precincts of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine}} in {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|1=Toyokawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633645}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|WD=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}.<ref name="uy">Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): p.17</ref> It Ranks fourth among the four subordinate shrines under the administration of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Edited by Ujiyamada City Hall (1929): pp.15, 17</ref> Unlike typical subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine, which usually do not have offertory boxes,<ref name="ic"/> Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has one, as shown in the photograph.
{{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}} and Shimo-no-Mii Shrine have similar structures.<ref name="jk4">Hozumi (2013): p.164</ref> The structure within the {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=Tamagaki}} (sacred fence) is not a shrine hall but an "覆屋" (covering structure) built over a well.<ref name="ic"/> The覆屋 at Kami-no-Mii Shrine is large enough for a priest to enter, whereas the覆屋 at Shimo-no-Mii Shrine is smaller and cannot be entered.<ref name="jk4"/> Additionally, while Kami-no-Mii Shrine has a double-layered sacred fence, Shimo-no-Mii Shrine has a single-layered fence corresponding to the smaller覆屋.<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.163-164</ref> Similar enclosed-type {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} have been excavated from {{ill|Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Mie)|lt=Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|ja|宝塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Kofun (Matsusaka)|qq=}} (in {{ill|Matsusaka|en|Matsusaka|WD=Q460829|lt=Matsusaka}}) and {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} (in {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|WD=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa}}).<ref name="jk4"/>
The enshrined deity is Shimo-no-Mii Mamorino-Kami (しものみいのまもりのかみ),<ref name="ic"/> regarded as the guardian deity of the sacred water of the Outer Shrine<ref name="gk"/> or the guardian of the well from which the sacred water is drawn, making it a {{ill|Suijin|en|Suijin|WD=Q3137449|lt=water god}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== History ==
The date of establishment is unknown. Although there is no mention of the shrine itself in the "Toyouke-no-Miya Gishiki-chō," references to "御井" (sacred well) and "御井神" (god of the sacred well) are believed to refer to Kami-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="jk2">Hozumi (2013): p.162</ref> The text also refers to "御井二所" (two sacred wells), which likely indicates Kami-no-Mii and Shimo-no-Mii.<ref name="jk2"/>
Until the modern era, {{ill|Takanomiya|ja|多賀宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takanomiya|1=Taka-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11431075}}, the highest-Ranked of the Outer Shrine's auxiliary shrines, had its own sacred kitchen (忌火屋殿) distinct from that of the Outer Shrine to prepare sacred offerings. However, during the Meiji period, the sacred offerings were consolidated at the Outer Shrine's sacred kitchen, and the sacred kitchen of Takanomiya was abolished.<ref>Sakurai (1998): pp.75-77</ref> The well used until the abolition remained and became Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref name="ij"/>
Shimo-no-Mii is considered a backup well for {{ill|Kami-no-Mii Shrine|ja|上御井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-no-Mii Shrine|qq=}},<ref name="gk"/><ref name="ic"/> though there has reportedly never been an instance of Kami-no-Mii drying up.<ref>{{ill|iTV|ja|アイティービー|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/iTV|1=iTV|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11282246}} "[http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/s0003.php Shimo-no-Mii Shrine | Ise Grand Shrine Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoitokose]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref> However, the "Daijingu Shozatsujiki" notes that in the 5th year of {{ill|Eishō (Heian period)|en|Eishō (Heian period)|WD=Q1193549|lt=Eishō (Japanese era)}} (1050), Kami-no-Mii dried up, and water was drawn from Shimo-no-Mii located in front of {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="jk3">Hozumi (2013): p.163</ref>
In June of {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=Meiji 5}} (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|WD=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion}} transferred shrines not listed in the "Engishiki Jinmyōchō" or "Enryaku Gishiki-chō" from the administration of Ise Grand Shrine to the jurisdiction of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486130}} and Mie Prefecture, including Shimo-no-Mii Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp.266, 272</ref> In the following year (1873), the Shrine Bureau obtained permission to reinstate both Shimo-no-Mii Shrine and Kami-no-Mii Shrine as subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine.<ref>Sakurai (1991): p.272</ref> The覆屋 was rebuilt in March of {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=Taisho 1}} (1913).<ref name="uy"/>
== Access ==
The shrine is located slightly uphill in a valley near {{ill|Tsuchinomiya|ja|土宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchinomiya|1=Tsuchi-no-miya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11423156}}.<ref name="ic"/>
; Public Transportation * Approximately a 5-minute walk from {{ill|Iseshi Station|en|Iseshi Station|WD=Q6079748|lt=Ise-shi Station}} South Exit (JR side) on the {{ill|Sangū Line|en|Sangū Line|WD=Q872023|lt=Sangū Line}} (operated by {{ill|Central Japan Railway Company|en|Central Japan Railway Company|WD=Q513679|lt=JR Central}}) or the {{ill|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|en|Yamada Line (Kintetsu)|WD=Q900630|lt=Yamada Line (Kintetsu)}} (operated by {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}}).<ref>Edited by Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.33</ref> * A 5-minute walk from "Ise-shi Ekimae" Bus Stop (operated by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|WD=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu}}). * Immediately accessible from "Geku-mae" Bus Stop (operated by Mie Kotsu).
; By Car * About 5 minutes north from {{ill|Ise-nishi IC|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi IC|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|en|Ise Expressway|WD=Q6079578|lt=Ise Expressway}} via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mokimoto Road). There are parking lots nearby with a total capacity of 448 vehicles.<ref>{{ill|Jingu Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingu Administration Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}} "[http://www.isejingu.or.jp/mobile/i/parking/index.html Parking Guide]" (Accessed September 11, 2013)</ref>
